subject: Sports
title: Sneaker Culture and Air Jordans
description: One-minute skill: Sneaker Culture and Air Jordans.

hook.text: A pair of basketball shoes became the most valuable fashion item on Earth. Nike and Jordan made it happen.

buildup.text: In 1984, Nike signed rookie Michael Jordan and created the Air Jordan 1—a red and black sneaker.

discovery.text: The NBA banned the shoe for violating uniform rules. Nike paid the fines and used the ban as marketing gold.

twist.text: The Jordan Brand now earns over $5 billion annually—more than most NBA teams are worth.

climax.text: Sneaker culture expanded beyond basketball. Shoes became collectible art, fashion, and investment.

punchline.text: A banned shoe became the most valuable brand in sports history.

quiz.question: Why were the original Air Jordan 1 shoes notable?
options: The NBA banned them for violating uniform rules
They were the cheapest shoes available
They were designed by Michael Jordan himself
